target: build-scripts/generate-html.js + test.js — close out remaining EXP-256 c1/c2 holes (eclipse 'annular'→'total' factual fix at line 1754; verdict-bar aria-label denominator language fix at line 489; missing exactly-once test for ds-falsifiability-module; regression guards)

patches.rationale: Fixes the factual error at line 1754: the August 12, 2026 eclipse is total (NASA Besselian magnitude 1.0386 — already correctly stated at line 2949 of the same generated file), not annular. The annular eclipse of 2026 was February 17 (Antarctic). The falsifiability module's fm-jump anchor lands readers directly on this paragraph, so the error has elevated traffic and is a credibility attack surface for any defender. Closes EXP-256 c2 H3 / ISS-1718.

Disambiguates the screen-reader denominator collision flagged in EXP-256 c2 H1. The verdict-bar aria-labels currently read 'X of 71 claims' immediately above the falsifiability module which reads 'of 69 claims' — same noun, different denominator. Visible disambiguation already exists (EXP-271 added the bar caption '71 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims', and the section heading is 'How the 71 catalog entries actually land'). This patch aligns the bar aria-label noun to that visible language ('catalog entries') so screen-readers get the same disambiguation by element-nav. The module aria-labels keep 'claims' because there the 69 ARE the dome's claimed wins — preserving the linguistic distinction.

Add the missing exactly-once assertion for ds-falsifiability-module (proposal-spec test that EXP-271 didn't include — its assertion uses includes() which is at-least-one). Add regression guards for the P1 annular/total fix. Add an assertion that the verdict-bar aria-labels now use 'catalog entries' (matches visible caption noun) and not 'claims' (which collides with the module's denominator). All assertions tagged EXP-277 for grep clarity.
patches.anchor:
assert(htmlContent.includes("sub-0.01% precision over similar distances"), 'EXP-271: eg-falsifiability factual fix (sub-meter → sub-0.01%) present');
  assert(!htmlContent.includes("standard geodesy's sub-meter precision"), 'EXP-271: eg-falsifiability "sub-meter precision" wording removed');

holes_addressed: EXP-256 c2 H1 (ISS-1696 part 1) — verdict-bar aria-label denominator collision: bar said 'of 71 claims' adjacent to module's 'of 69 claims'. Screen-readers using element-nav heard same noun, different denominator, no inline explanation. Visible caption already disambiguates ('71 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims') from EXP-271; this patch aligns the bar aria-label noun to match. Module aria-labels keep 'claims' because the 69 are dome-claimed wins — preserving the linguistic distinction.

EXP-256 c2 H2 (ISS-1696 part 2 / ISS-1717) — proposal-spec exactly-once test for ds-falsifiability-module was not added in EXP-271. EXP-271 added 17 assertions including ds-fm-row count, condition titles, fm-jump href, framing, WCAG, and visual order, but its existence check uses includes() which is at-least-one. This patch adds the missing exactly-once assertion using a regex match-count.

EXP-256 c2 H3 (ISS-1696 part 3 / ISS-1695 part 2 / ISS-1718) — eg-falsifiability eclipse pre-commitment paragraph at line 1754 of generate-html.js (line 673 of docs/index.html) said 'August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse'. Section 2.7 (line 2949) correctly says 'total, not annular' with NASA Besselian magnitude 1.0386. Annular eclipse of 2026 was February 17 (Antarctic). Module's fm-jump anchor lands readers on this paragraph so the error had elevated traffic.

EXP-256 c2 H4 (ISS-1696 part 4) — '0/69' framing conflated did-not-attempt with attempted-and-failed. RESOLVED by EXP-271 (now reads '0 / N met' headline plus '2 attempted · 0 succeed' / 'imports globe radius' / 'OPEN-001' sub-statuses). No new patch needed; included here for closure tracking.

EXP-256 c2 H5 (ISS-1696 part 5) — visual order: thesis-hero appearing AFTER falsifiability module. RESOLVED by EXP-271 + EXP-261 v2 reordering (now thesis-hero → verdict-bars → falsifiability-module, asserted by EXP-271 position tests at test.js lines 716-724). No new patch needed; included here for closure tracking.

EXP-256 c1 H1 (ISS-1694) — WCAG contrast for fm-status-num red on --card-bg in dark mode. RESOLVED by EXP-271 (font-size bumped to 1.2rem to clear AA large-text threshold; sub-status uses --ink-2). No new patch needed; included here for closure tracking.

EXP-256 c1 H2-H3 (ISS-1695 part 1) — asymmetric condition framing for conditions 3/4. RESOLVED by EXP-271 (consistent '0 / N met' framing across conditions 1/3/4; OPEN-001 sub-status carries condition 4 nuance, replacing standalone 'pending' word). No new patch needed; included here for closure tracking.
new_evidence_added: NASA Besselian elements for August 12, 2026 — magnitude 1.0386 (>1 = total) — already cited at docs/index.html line 2949 in Section 2.7. P1 brings line 673 (eg-falsifiability) into agreement with Section 2.7.

Visible bar caption from EXP-271: '${total} catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims' — this anchors the linguistic distinction. P2 propagates that distinction into bar aria-labels.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon recommended a parenthetical on fm-caption to bridge the 71/69 gap. P2 instead changes verdict-bar aria-label noun. Why diverge?
anticipated_objections.response: Visible disambiguation already exists from EXP-271 (the bar caption literally enumerates '71 = 69 + 2'). The remaining gap is screen-reader element-nav, where users land on a bar aria-label without hearing the caption first. Changing the bar aria-label noun to match the visible caption noun ('catalog entries') is a one-character class fix that closes that gap. Adding fm-caption clutter when the visible disambiguation already exists is the higher-cost option.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not change the falsifiability module's aria-labels to 'wins' or 'dome claims' instead?
anticipated_objections.response: The module's denominator IS 'dome-claimed wins' (per fm-caption: 'Four conditions assessed against the dome's 69 claimed wins'). 'Claims' is the correct noun for the module. The verdict-bar's denominator is catalog entries (3-digit IDs + sub-claim collisions), so 'catalog entries' is the correct noun there. Changing the module's noun would introduce a new mismatch, not close one.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 'annular' / 'total' fix is just a typo — does it warrant a full EXP?
anticipated_objections.response: On its own, no. Bundled with the test additions and aria-label disambiguation it's the right shape: EXP-277 closes out all five remaining EXP-256 c1/c2 holes plus the duplicate ISS-1717/ISS-1718 from EXP-280 in one coordinated patch. The factual error is the highest-stakes piece because the module's fm-jump anchor drives readers directly to that paragraph and a defender can use 'they don't even know what kind of eclipse it is' as a credibility attack.

anticipated_objections.objection: The aria-label change ripples across every verdict-bar element. Could that introduce regression?
anticipated_objections.response: The change is a single-occurrence string replace inside one template literal. The visible verdict-bar text (count, percentage, label) is unchanged — only the aria-label noun. The new test asserts the new noun is present and the old noun is gone, so any regression is caught at test time. Build-time impact: zero (no layout, no CSS, no DOM structure).

anticipated_objections.objection: Why subsume EXP-280 rather than work it independently?
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-280's two issues (ISS-1717 test gap, ISS-1718 eg-falsifiability factual error) are the SAME holes as EXP-256 c2 H2 and H3. EXP-280 was created from the EXP-256-proposal review, EXP-277 from the EXP-256-section-new review — same source artifact, same holes, different reviewer queue runs. Coordinated patch closes all five holes in one decider integration cycle. Subsumed_by=EXP-277 set on EXP-280 in tracker.

anticipated_objections.objection: If EXP-271 already fixed most of c1/c2, what's the point of EXP-277? Just close the issues.
anticipated_objections.response: Two of the seven c1/c2 holes (H3 annular, H2 missing exactly-once test) genuinely remain open and require a code patch. The other five (H1 denominator, H4 0/69 framing, H5 visual order, c1 H1 WCAG, c1 H2-H3 framing) are resolved-but-tracked. EXP-277 patches the two open holes and provides an audit trail for the five resolved ones so the decider can close all five issues with reference to a single EXP.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon's EXP-256 c1/c2 reviews caught real holes — the original integration shipped with several rough edges (CSS prefix, WCAG, framing, visual order, denominator language). EXP-271 already addressed the structural ones in a coordinated patch. What survived is two genuinely substantive items: a factual error in the eg-falsifiability paragraph the module routes traffic to (annular vs total — these are different physical phenomena), and a missing 'exactly once' test that the proposal listed but EXP-271 didn't include. The aria-label disambiguation is the smallest fix that closes the screen-reader version of the denominator-mismatch concern without adding visible clutter to a page that EXP-271 already disambiguated visibly. The 'kernel of truth' on EXP-256 itself is that surfacing four explicit falsification conditions on the Overview tab is real value — the corrections close defender attack surfaces without dimming that value.
test_validation: After patches apply, the following assertions hold: (1) docs/index.html contains 'the August 12, 2026 total solar eclipse' exactly once and does not contain 'the August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse'; (2) verdict-bar aria-labels match /aria-label="[^"]*: \d+ of \d+ catalog entries \(/; (3) ds-falsifiability-module class appears exactly once in docs/index.html. New EXP-277 assertions: 5. Total test count expected: prior baseline + 5.

next_run_guidance.analyst: Mode 1 next run: EXP-278 (§4.2.3 ambiguous 3.6× phrasing + strawman framing) is next-up. Two moderate editorial holes from EXP-253 c1; lower priority than EXP-277 was. Mode 0 likely 71/71. Mode 2b/3/4 quiet.
next_run_guidance.decider: Five ISS resolution proposals queued (ISS-1694, ISS-1695, ISS-1696, ISS-1717, ISS-1718). Patches are 1× exact_string_replace in generate-html.js (annular→total), 1× exact_string_replace in generate-html.js (verdict-bar aria-label noun), 1× insert_after_anchor in test.js (5 new EXP-277 assertions). Run tests/run.sh on integration. Close all five ISS with reference to EXP-277. Mark EXP-277 integrated; EXP-280 already has subsumed_by=EXP-277 set in tracker so close it via that reference. ISS-1694, ISS-1695 c1 portions, c2 H4 and H5: close as 'resolved by EXP-271' with cross-reference to EXP-277 for audit trail. Spot-check that the visible 'annular' string in docs/index.html line 673 becomes 'total' after rebuild.
next_run_guidance.curmudgeon: After integration, c1 spot-check could verify (i) eg-falsifiability now reads 'total solar eclipse' for August 12, 2026 and matches Section 2.7 verbatim on the eclipse type, (ii) verdict-bar aria-labels' new 'catalog entries' noun does not break the existing screen-reader test pattern (no other test depends on the prior 'claims' string), (iii) the new exactly-once test correctly counts ds-falsifiability-module once on the Overview tab and does not double-count if a future patch surfaces the module on a second tab. No deeper review needed — the patches are mechanical and the holes addressed are tracked-vs-resolved against existing EXP-271 work.
